title: [Transforms] add a `memref` flattening pass
body:
This is a pass which flattens statically sized memories and indexing into them by elaborating multi-dimensional indexing into a tree of multipliers, shifts, and adders. The motivation of this is to be able to lower programs which have multi-dimensional memories through the handshake flow that currently only supports single-dimensional memories when lowering to FIRRTL.

This also creates a "Transforms" CIRCT library, following upstream MLIR conventions.

The PR is marked as draft due to an issue which I can't seem to solve. The `rewriter.convertRegionTypes` throws a memory exception deep within a `DenseMap` function. While i assume the fault is on my side, I've yet to figure out what is actually going wrong. The type conversion approach applied here is similar to what is done elsewhere. I'd very much appreciate if somebody could check out the PR on their end and see if the error can be reproduced.
